text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic D-III lymph node dissection while preserving the left colic artery and sigmoid artery in a patient with rectal cancer.

The surgeon performs a laparoscopic exploration, carefully dissociating the retroperitoneum from the right side of the mesocolon. The Toltz gap is entered along the right side of the inferior mesenteric artery.
The surgeon performs a laparoscopic dissection of apical lymph node number 253, carefully isolating it from surrounding tissues.

The surgeon dissociates and exposes the inferior mesenteric artery and vein, as well as the left colic artery. Apical lymph node number 253 is dissected. The branches of the inferior mesenteric vein are ligated and incised to protect the left colic artery.

The surgeon ligates and incises the inferior mesenteric vein, followed by dissociation and exposure of the first sigmoid artery. The inferior mesenteric artery is then ligated and incised below the left colic artery.
The surgeon places gauze to protect the left ureter and gonadal vessels, then proceeds to separate adhesions of the sigmoid colon and dissociate the sigmoid mesocolon.
The surgeon dissociates the retro-rectal space and the right pararectal space, carefully separating these anatomical areas during the rectal cancer procedure.

The surgeon dissociates the left pararectal space, then proceeds to dissociate and expose the right intestinal wall, anterior rectal wall, and left intestinal wall, revealing these anatomical structures in the surgical field.
The surgeon incises the rectum at the pre-resect position and proceeds to incise the sigmoid mesocolon. The specimen is extracted through the incision made for the protective ileostomy.
The surgeon performs an enteric and intestinal anastomosis, followed by suturing to strengthen the anastomotic site.
The surgeon places a drainage tube and creates a stoma at the incision site. The specimen, a total mesorectal excision, is visible.
The pathologic staging was PT-III-N-I-M-Zero, with one apical lymph node metastasis.
